_meta.note: Curated skill sequences for common operator use cases. Each step is a vetted skill plus the reason it belongs in the chain. Cross-reference token-budget.json to estimate context cost.

recipes.name: AI Red Team Engagement Prep
recipes.description: Stand up the AI security baseline before red-teaming a model or AI feature. Covers attack surface, MCP plugin trust, prompt-injection class, agentic actions, RAG provenance.
recipes.when_to_use: Before scoping or executing a red-team engagement against a model, agentic system, or AI feature.

steps.why: Comprehensive attack-surface inventory mapped to ATLAS v5.1.0 with gap flags.
Detection coverage for AI-as-C2 (PROMPTFLUX / SesameOp / AI-API egress) before testing.
MCP server trust boundary for the engineering toolchain side of the surface.
RAG ingestion provenance + prompt-injection chain coverage.
Frame the engagement scope using a current methodology (PASTA/LINDDUN-AI variant).

recipes.name: PCI DSS 4.0 Audit Defense
recipes.description: Show real coverage of PCI 4.0 sections that auditors most commonly mis-read as 'compliant'. Focus on 6.3.3, 11.4, 12.10.
recipes.when_to_use: Mock audit, ROC drafting, or QSA pre-engagement review.

steps.why: Detect the 7 documented theater patterns that pass PCI but leave exposure.
Pre-analyzed PCI control gaps and what to substitute as compensating controls.
PCI 12.10 incident-response coverage that maps to ATT&CK + AI-class incidents.
PCI 6.4 covers SDLC; supply-chain integrity covers what 6.4 omits (MCP / coding assistants).

recipes.name: Federal Incident Response (FISMA + CISA + FedRAMP)
recipes.description: Coordinate an incident affecting a federal system. Layered SLAs: CISA 8h, OMB M-22-09 access-control breach, FedRAMP continuous-monitoring obligations.
recipes.when_to_use: Active or suspected incident on a federal-system boundary, FedRAMP CSP, or DoD/CMMC scope.

steps.why: PICERL + cross-jurisdiction notification clocks (CISA 8h initial, OMB, FedRAMP).
Federal-specific framework obligations (NIST 800-53, FedRAMP, CMMC, OMB memos).
If a vulnerability triggered the incident, CVD coordination via CISA's process.
Time-bound exceptions for any control bypassed under emergency response.

recipes.name: DORA TLPT Scoping (EU Financial Services)
recipes.description: Scope a Threat-Led Penetration Test under DORA Art. 26-27 for an in-scope EU financial entity.
recipes.when_to_use: Annual or triennial DORA TLPT scoping cycle, or after a critical-third-party change.

steps.why: DORA-specific scoping requirements (Art. 26-27) and critical-function inventory.
Pen-test methodology aligned to ATT&CK Enterprise + financial-services adversary profile.
Threat-led methodology (ATT&CK + ATLAS variants), red-team rules of engagement.
ICT third-party scope (DORA Art. 28-30) — TLPT must cover critical ICT third parties.
Findings → DORA Art. 19 major-incident reporting (4h / 24h / 30d) if a real incident is uncovered.

recipes.name: K-12 EdTech Privacy & Safety Review
recipes.description: Review an EdTech product or pilot for a K-12 deployment. COPPA, FERPA, UK Children's Code, EU AI Act Annex III, AU eSafety, age-appropriate design.
recipes.when_to_use: Procurement review for an EdTech vendor, K-12 board approval cycle, or post-deployment audit.

steps.why: Age-appropriate design code, age estimation/assurance, dark-pattern audit, AI Act Annex III.
Student record / PII flow audit; FERPA + state student-privacy laws (CA SOPIPA, NY Ed Law 2-D).
Auth posture for child accounts — NIST 800-63B IAL2/AAL2 mismatch for age-restricted UIs.
K-12 vendor attestations are a known theater hotspot; surface the gaps.
If the EdTech includes any AI/LLM features (the common case), RAG + prompt-injection class.

recipes.name: Ransomware Tabletop Exercise
recipes.description: Run a tabletop covering modern ransomware: double-extortion, data exfiltration before encryption, OT impact, AI-assisted negotiation messages, multi-jurisdictional notification.
recipes.when_to_use: Annual tabletop, post-incident replay, or executive-team readiness review.

steps.why: PICERL + cross-jurisdiction breach notification (GDPR 72h, NIS2 24h/72h, US state laws, NYDFS 72h).
If financial-sector entity: ransom-payment disclosure (NYDFS 24h), DORA Art. 19, SAMA, MAS TRM.
If OT/critical infrastructure exposure: CIRCIA 24h, NERC CIP, IEC 62443, recovery without paying.
Initial access vector typical of ransomware; AI-generated phishing baseline.
Map exercise findings to D3FEND countermeasures with verifiable telemetry.

recipes.name: New CVE Drop Triage
recipes.description: Standardized first 30 minutes after a high-impact CVE drop. RWEP score, KEV check, evidence chain, dispatched to the right specialized skill.
recipes.when_to_use: Operator receives a CVE number from an advisory, news item, or vendor notice and needs to know what to do.

steps.why: Entry-point dispatcher: anchors the CVE in the catalog, computes RWEP, routes to the right specialist.
RWEP score + factor breakdown, KEV + EPSS posture, weaponization timeline estimate.
Pre-analyzed control gaps that auditor-track frameworks have for this CVE class.
If active exploitation suspected: notification clocks + PICERL kickoff.

recipes.name: Open-Source Dependency Triage
recipes.description: Triage a new advisory in a transitive npm/PyPI/Maven dependency: scope, SBOM, exploit-availability, action.
recipes.when_to_use: Dependabot or Renovate fires a critical/high finding, or a public advisory mentions a dependency you ship.

steps.why: SBOM scoping, transitive-dep evaluation, build-system trust, signed-attestation check.
RWEP + EPSS + KEV decide whether this is a 'drop everything' or 'normal cycle' patch.
If the advisory is pre-coordination, hold the disclosure until the upstream window closes.
If you maintain a fork or critical patch: prioritized fuzz target the bug class.
